label: Legacy Reaction-Recurred Field (non-standard)

backend.evidence: DrugReactionAssessment.reaction_recurred (values 1-3) is a non-standard field: its enum does not match the official G.k.9.i.4 (1-4) and no G.k.9.i.4.r.3 element exists in any dictionary or in the FDA/KR reference instances.

frontend.evidence: No frontend input; the official rechallenge value is captured by recurrenceAction (G.k.9.i.4).

action: Backend cleanup candidate: remove reaction_recurred or fold into recurrence_action; not an E2BR3 element.
notes: Phantom backend field retained only so the strict backend inventory stays complete. Superseded by the G.k.9.i.4 mapping on recurrence_action.

label: Did Reaction Recur on Re-administration?

backend.evidence: DrugReactionAssessment.recurrence_action holds the official 1-4 enum (1=rechallenge done & recurred ... 4=no rechallenge/NA), matching the FDA reference instance observation code=31 / value codeSystem ...2.1.1.16 annotated G.k.9.i.4.

frontend.evidence: SectionG renders the E2BRadioField labelled 'Did Reaction Recur on Re-administration?' fieldNumber G.k.9.i.4 with the 1-4 options, bound to drugReactionAssessments[assessIndex].recurrenceAction.

notes: Re-pointed 2026-06 after evidence review: the official single 1-4 element G.k.9.i.4 is implemented by recurrence_action/recurrenceAction (values, label and FDA reference-instance representation all match). The backend field name says 'action' but functionally holds the official value; rename is optional cleanup, not required for correctness.

label: FDA Device Info CommonDeviceName

backend.evidence: DrugInformation.fda_device_info_json is a single JSON carrier for multiple nested FDA device frontend inputs.

frontend.evidence: Strict frontend input inventory extracts drugs.fdaDeviceInfo.commonDeviceName from the case-form section source.

action: Keep this as an explicit JSON-carrier conflict until the backend exposes first-class BMC fields or the registry supports nested JSON schemas.
notes: Frontend field is real, but it does not have a 1-1 backend BMC field because the backend stores the object in fda_device_info_json. Renamed to the official FDA regional device element; backend still stores this in the DrugInformation.fda_device_info_json carrier, hence the conflict status. (was commonDeviceName in the JSON carrier).

label: FDA Device Info Malfunction

backend.evidence: DrugInformation.fda_device_info_json is a single JSON carrier for multiple nested FDA device frontend inputs.

frontend.evidence: Strict frontend input inventory extracts drugs.fdaDeviceInfo.malfunction from the case-form section source.

action: Keep this as an explicit JSON-carrier conflict until the backend exposes first-class BMC fields or the registry supports nested JSON schemas.
notes: Frontend field is real, but it does not have a 1-1 backend BMC field because the backend stores the object in fda_device_info_json. Renamed to the official FDA regional device element; backend still stores this in the DrugInformation.fda_device_info_json carrier, hence the conflict status. (was malfunction in the JSON carrier).

label: FDA Other Characterisation of Drug Role

backend.evidence: DrugInformation.fda_other_characterization (VARCHAR(10), '1'=Similar Device) persists FDA.G.k.1.a via the drug INSERT/UPDATE binds.

frontend.evidence: SectionG renders an FDA-gated E2BRadioField (fieldNumber FDA.G.k.1.a, option 1=Similar Device) bound to drugs[index].fdaOtherCharacterization.

notes: Implemented 2026-06: FDA Other Characterisation of Drug Role (post-market conditional-required when combination product + malfunction). Single coded value 1=Similar Device.
